# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment
We want this project to be a welcoming, respectful place for everyone.
Contributors, maintainers, and community members are expected to collaborate in good faith.

## Expected Behavior
- Be respectful and constructive in discussions.
- Assume good intent and ask clarifying questions before escalating.
- Give actionable feedback focused on code, docs, and outcomes.
- Accept responsibility for mistakes and correct them quickly.
- Help keep issues and pull requests on-topic.

## Unacceptable Behavior
- Harassment, intimidation, threats, or discriminatory language.
- Personal attacks, trolling, or sustained disruptive behavior.
- Publishing private information without explicit permission.
- Sexualized language or unwanted sexual attention.
- Any behavior that a reasonable person would find hostile or abusive.

## Scope
This Code of Conduct applies to all project spaces, including issues, pull requests, discussions, and related communication channels.

## Reporting
If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, report it privately to the maintainers.
Include links, screenshots, and context so maintainers can investigate quickly.

## Enforcement
Maintainers may take any action they deem appropriate, including warning, temporary restrictions, or permanent removal from project spaces.

## Attribution
This document is inspired by common open source community standards and adapted for this repository.
